sections.label: Section 342
sections.original: οὐκοῦν ἅ γʼ ἥξει καὶ σὲ χρὴ λέγειν ἐμοί .
sections.translation: Since it must come anyway , it is right that you tell it to me .

sections.label: Section 343
sections.original: οὐκ ἂν πέρα φράσαιμι . πρὸς τάδʼ , εἰ θέλεις ,
sections.translation: I will speak no further : rage , if you wish , with the fiercest wrath your heart knows .

sections.label: Section 345
sections.original: καὶ μὴν παρήσω γʼ οὐδέν , ὡς ὀργῆς ἔχω ,
sections.translation: In my anger I will not spare to speak all my thoughts . Know that you seem to me to have helped in plotting the deed , and to have done it , short of performing the actual murder with your own hands : if you had eyesight , I would have said that you had done even this by yourself .

sections.label: Section 350
sections.original: ἄληθες ; ἐννέπω σὲ τῷ κηρύγματι
sections.translation: In truth ? I order you to abide by you own decree , and from this day forth not to speak to these men or to me : you are the accursed defiler of this land .

sections.label: Section 354
sections.original: οὕτως ἀναιδῶς ἐξεκίνησας τόδε
sections.translation: So brazen with your blustering taunt ?

sections.label: Section 355
sections.original: τὸ ῥῆμα ; καὶ ποῦ τοῦτο φεύξεσθαι δοκεῖς ;
sections.translation: Where do you think to escape to ?

sections.label: Section 356
sections.original: πέφευγα · τἀληθὲς γὰρ ἰσχῦον τρέφω .
sections.translation: I have escaped . There is strength in my truth .

sections.label: Section 357
sections.original: πρὸς τοῦ διδαχθείς ; οὐ γὰρ ἔκ γε τῆς τέχνης .
sections.translation: Who taught you this ? Not your skill , at any rate .

sections.label: Section 358
sections.original: πρὸς σοῦ · σὺ γάρ μʼ ἄκοντα προυτρέψω λέγειν .
sections.translation: You yourself . For you spurred me on to speak against my will .

sections.label: Section 359
sections.original: ποῖον λόγον ; λέγʼ αὖθις , ὡς μᾶλλον μάθω .
sections.translation: What did you say ? Speak again , so I may learn it better .

sections.label: Section 360
sections.original: οὐχὶ ξυνῆκας πρόσθεν ; ἢ ʼκπειρᾷ λέγων ;
sections.translation: Did you not understand before , or are you talking to test me ?

sections.label: Section 361
sections.original: οὐχ ὥστε γʼ εἰπεῖν γνωστόν · ἀλλʼ αὖθις φράσον .
sections.translation: I cannot say I understood fully . Tell me again .

sections.label: Section 362
sections.original: φονέα σε φημὶ τἀνδρὸς οὗ ζητεῖς κυρεῖν .
sections.translation: I say that you are the killer of the man whose slayer you seek .

sections.label: Section 363
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὔ τι χαίρων δίς γε πημονὰς ἐρεῖς .
sections.translation: Now you will regret that you have said such dire words twice .

sections.label: Section 364
sections.original: εἴπω τι δῆτα κἄλλʼ , ἵνʼ ὀργίζῃ πλέον ;
sections.translation: Should I tell you more , that you might get more angry ?

sections.label: Section 365
sections.original: ὅσον γε χρῄζεις · ὡς μάτην εἰρήσεται .
sections.translation: Say as much as you want : it will be said in vain .

sections.label: Section 366
sections.original: λεληθέναι σε φημὶ σὺν τοῖς φιλτάτοις
sections.translation: I say that you have been living in unguessed shame with your closest kin , and do not see into what woe you have fallen .

sections.label: Section 368
sections.original: ἦ καὶ γεγηθὼς ταῦτʼ ἀεὶ λέξειν δοκεῖς ;
sections.translation: Do you think that you will always be able to speak like this without smarting for it ?

sections.label: Section 369
sections.original: εἴπερ τί γʼ ἐστὶ τῆς ἀληθείας σθένος .
sections.translation: Yes , if indeed there is any strength in truth .

sections.label: Section 370
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἔστι , πλὴν σοί · σοὶ δὲ τοῦτʼ οὐκ ἔστʼ ἐπεὶ
sections.translation: But there is , except not for you . You do not have that strength , since you are maimed in your ears , in your wit , and in your eyes .

sections.label: Section 372
sections.original: σὺ δʼ ἄθλιός γε ταῦτʼ ὀνειδίζων , ἃ σοὶ
sections.translation: And you are a poor wretch to utter taunts that every man here will soon hurl at you .

sections.label: Section 374
sections.original: μιᾶς τρέφει πρὸς νυκτός , ὥστε μήτʼ ἐμὲ
sections.translation: Night , endless night has you in her keeping , so that you can never hurt me ,

sections.label: Section 375
sections.original: μήτʼ ἄλλον , ὅστις φῶς ὁρᾷ , βλάψαι ποτʼ ἄν .
sections.translation: or any man that sees the light of the sun .

sections.label: Section 376
sections.original: οὐ γάρ σε μοῖρα πρός γʼ ἐμοῦ πεσεῖν , ἐπεὶ
sections.translation: No , it is not your fate to fall at my hands , since Apollo , to whom this matter is a concern , is sufficient .

sections.label: Section 378
sections.original: Κρέοντος ἢ σοῦ ταῦτα τἀξευρήματα ;
sections.translation: Are these Creon ’ s devices , or your own ?

sections.label: Section 379
sections.original: Κρέων δέ σοι πῆμʼ οὐδέν , ἀλλʼ αὐτὸς σὺ σοί .
sections.translation: Creon is no trouble for you : you are your own .

sections.label: Section 380
sections.original: ὦ πλοῦτε καὶ τυραννὶ καὶ τέχνη τέχνης
sections.translation: O wealth , and empire , and skill surpassing skill in life ’ s keen rivalries , how great is the envy in your keeping , if for the sake of this office which the city has entrusted to me , a gift unsought ,

sections.label: Section 385
sections.original: ταύτης Κρέων ὁ πιστός , οὑξ ἀρχῆς φίλος ,
sections.translation: Creon the trustworthy , Creon , my old friend , has crept upon me by stealth , yearning to overthrow me , and has suborned such a scheming juggler as this , a tricky quack , who has eyes only for profit , but is blind in his art !

sections.label: Section 390
sections.original: ἐπεί , φέρʼ εἰπέ , ποῦ σὺ μάντις εἶ σαφής ;
sections.translation: Come , tell me , where have you proved yourself a seer ? Why , when the watchful dog who wove dark song was here , did you say nothing to free the people ? Yet the riddle , at least , was not for the first comer to read : there was need of a seer ’ s help ,

sections.label: Section 395
sections.original: ἣν οὔτʼ ἀπʼ οἰωνῶν σὺ προυφάνης ἔχων
sections.translation: and you were discovered not to have this art , either from birds , or known from some god . But rather I , Oedipus the ignorant , stopped her , having attained the answer through my wit alone , untaught by birds . It is I whom you are trying to oust , assuming that

sections.label: Section 400
sections.original: παραστατήσειν τοῖς Κρεοντείοις πέλας .
sections.translation: you will have great influence in Creon ’ s court . But I think that you and the one who plotted these things will rue your zeal to purge the land : if you did not seem to be an old man , you would have learned to your cost how haughty you are .

sections.label: Section 404
sections.original: ἡμῖν μὲν εἰκάζουσι καὶ τὰ τοῦδʼ ἔπη
sections.translation: To our way of thinking , these words , both his and yours , Oedipus ,

sections.label: Section 405
sections.original: ὀργῇ λελέχθαι καὶ τά σʼ , Οἰδίπους , δοκεῖ ,
sections.translation: have been said in anger . We have no need of this , but rather we must seek how we shall best discharge the mandates of the god .

sections.label: Section 408
sections.original: εἰ καὶ τυραννεῖς , ἐξισωτέον τὸ γοῦν
sections.translation: Though you are king , the right of reply must be considered the same for both : over that I have control .

sections.label: Section 410
sections.original: οὐ γάρ τι σοὶ ζῶ δοῦλος , ἀλλὰ Λοξίᾳ ·
sections.translation: For I do not live as your slave , but as Loxias ’. I will not stand enrolled as Creon ’ s client . And I tell you , since you have taunted my blindness , that though you have sight , you do not see what a state of misery you are in , or where you dwell , or with whom .

sections.label: Section 415
sections.original: ἆρʼ οἶσθʼ ἀφʼ ὧν εἶ ; καὶ λέληθας ἐχθρὸς ὢν
sections.translation: Do you know who your parents are ? You have been an unwitting enemy to your own kin , both in the Underworld and on the earth above , and the double lash of your mother ’ s and your father ’ s curse will one day drive you from this land in dreadful haste , with darkness upon those eyes of yours which now can see .

sections.label: Section 420
sections.original: βοῆς δὲ τῆς σῆς ποῖος οὐκ ἔσται λιμήν ,
sections.translation: What place will be harbor to your cries , what part of all Cithaeron will not ring with them soon , when you have learned the meaning of the nuptials in which , within that house , you found a fatal haven , after a voyage so fair ? And you have not guessed at a throng of other ills

sections.label: Section 425
sections.original: ἅ σʼ ἐξισώσει σοί τε καὶ τοῖς σοῖς τέκνοις .
sections.translation: which will bring you level with your true self and with your own children . Therefore heap your scorn upon Creon and upon my message : for no man will ever be crushed more miserably than you .

sections.label: Section 429
sections.original: ἦ ταῦτα δῆτʼ ἀνεκτὰ πρὸς τούτου κλύειν ;
sections.translation: Are these taunts to be endured from him ?

sections.label: Section 430
sections.original: οὐκ εἰς ὄλεθρον ; οὐχὶ θᾶσσον ; οὐ πάλιν
sections.translation: Be gone , to your ruin ; be gone this instant ! Will you not turn your back and leave this house ?

sections.label: Section 432
sections.original: οὐδʼ ἱκόμην ἔγωγʼ ἄν , εἰ σὺ μὴ ʼκάλεις .
sections.translation: I would not have come if you had not called me .

sections.label: Section 433
sections.original: οὐ γάρ τί σʼ ᾔδη μῶρα φωνήσοντʼ , ἐπεὶ
sections.translation: I did not know you would speak foolishly , for otherwise it would have been a long time before I summoned you to my home .

sections.label: Section 435
sections.original: ἡμεῖς τοιοίδʼ ἔφυμεν , ὡς μὲν σοὶ δοκεῖ ,
sections.translation: I was born like this — as you think , a fool , but in the opinion of the parents who bore you , quite sane .

sections.label: Section 437
sections.original: ποίοισι ; μεῖνον , τίς δέ μʼ ἐκφύει βροτῶν ;
sections.translation: What parents ? Wait . What man is my father ?

sections.label: Section 438
sections.original: ἥδʼ ἡμέρα φύσει σε καὶ διαφθερεῖ .
sections.translation: This day will reveal your birth and bring your ruin .

sections.label: Section 439
sections.original: ὡς πάντʼ ἄγαν αἰνικτὰ κἀσαφῆ λέγεις .
sections.translation: What riddles , what dark words you always say .

sections.label: Section 440
sections.original: οὔκουν σὺ ταῦτʼ ἄριστος εὑρίσκειν ἔφυς ;
sections.translation: Are you not the best at unravelling mysteries ?

sections.label: Section 441
sections.original: τοιαῦτʼ ὀνείδιζʼ , οἷς ἔμʼ εὑρήσεις μέγαν .
sections.translation: Reproach me in what you will find me to be great .

sections.label: Section 442
sections.original: αὕτη γε μέντοι σʼ ἡ τύχη διώλεσεν .
sections.translation: Yet it was just that fortune that undid you .

sections.label: Section 443
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εἰ πόλιν τήνδʼ ἐξέσωσʼ , οὔ μοι μέλει .
sections.translation: But if it saved this city I care not .

sections.label: Section 444
sections.original: ἄπειμι τοίνυν · καὶ σύ , παῖ , κόμιζέ με .
sections.translation: I will take my leave . You , boy , lead me .

sections.label: Section 445
sections.original: κομιζέτω δῆθʼ · ὡς παρὼν σύ γʼ ἐμποδὼν
sections.translation: Yes , let him take you : while here , you are a hindrance , a source of trouble . When you have gone , you will vex me no more .

sections.label: Section 447
sections.original: εἰπὼν ἄπειμʼ ὧν οὕνεκʼ , ἦλθον , οὐ τὸ σὸν
sections.translation: I will go when I have performed the errand for which I came , fearless of your frown : you can never destroy me . I tell you : the man whom you have been seeking this long while ,

sections.label: Section 450
sections.original: ζητεῖς ἀπειλῶν κἀνακηρύσσων φόνον
sections.translation: uttering threats and proclaiming a search into the murder of Laius , is here , ostensibly an alien sojourner , but soon to be found a native of Thebes ; nor will he enjoy his fortune . A blind man , though now he sees ,

sections.label: Section 455
sections.original: καὶ πτωχὸς ἀντὶ πλουσίου ξένην ἔπι
sections.translation: a beggar , though now rich , he will make his way to a foreign land , feeling the ground before him with his staff . And he will be discovered to be at once brother and father of the children with whom he consorts ; son and husband of the woman who bore him ;

sections.label: Section 460
sections.original: ὁμόσπορός τε καὶ φονεύς . καὶ ταῦτʼ ἰὼν
sections.translation: heir to his father ’ s bed , shedder of his father ’ s blood . So go in and evaluate this , and if you find that I am wrong , say then that I have no wit in prophecy .

sections.label: Section 1.463
sections.original: τίς ὅντινʼ ἁ θεσπιέπεια Δελφὶς εἶπε πέτρα
sections.translation: Who is he of whom the divine voice from the Delphian rock has said

sections.label: Section 1.465
sections.original: ἄρρητʼ ἀρρήτων τελέσαντα φοινίαισι χερσίν ;
sections.translation: to have wrought with blood-red hands horrors that no tongue can tell ? It is time that he ply in flight a foot stronger than the feet of storm-swift steeds .

sections.label: Section 1.470
sections.original: πυρὶ καὶ στεροπαῖς ὁ Διὸς γενέτας ,
sections.translation: The son of Zeus is springing upon him with fiery lightning , and with him come the dread unerring Fates .

sections.label: Section 1.473
sections.original: ἔλαμψε γὰρ τοῦ νιφόεντος ἀρτίως φανεῖσα
sections.translation: Recently the message has flashed forth from snowy Parnassus
